boundary: Lab-test only synthetic golden migration dataset; not production-ready, customer-ready, migration-approved, or suitable for customer data.

fixture_families.style: precast industrial materials workflow analog with concrete washout, wastewater, solids, chain-of-custody, batch chemistry, and signed COA expectations
workflow: receive multi-container industrial sample set under one synthetic chain-of-custody
expand profile into pH, TSS, alkalinity, and metals analysis request lines
assign wet chemistry and metals worksheet batches with method blank and LCS controls
review batch QC before report release
produce COA package with custody summary and analyte table

fixture_families.style: municipal environmental water workflow analog with routine compliance samples, field measurements, microbiology placeholder, and EDD-style export expectations
workflow: receive chilled drinking-water and wastewater grab samples
preserve container metadata and sampled/received timestamps
expand nutrient, field, metals, and microbiology placeholder services
produce client portal result summary plus machine-readable EDD rows

fixture_families.style: materials forensics workflow analog with air/filter/bulk samples, microscopy-style qualitative findings, subcontract flags, and narrative report expectations
workflow: receive batch of filter and bulk material samples with project-level custody
create per-sample analysis lines for gravimetric, microscopy, and metals services
track subcontract-required line without treating it as complete
review analyst narrative and QC duplicate agreement
produce narrative report package with table, comments, and attachment manifest

samples.preservation: received chilled with preserved chemistry containers

checks: method blank is below reporting limit
microbiology placeholder is not released as production claim
EDD rows reconcile to imported samples
subcontract flag blocks final release until resolved

expected_parity_gaps.current_behavior: client import currently migrates client name/email only; contacts and report-recipient roles remain fixture-only expectations
sample, container, preservation, and chain-of-custody rows are not yet importable through the generic migration path
analysis services, result values, qualifiers, and method snapshots are documented in the fixture but not migrated by ImportForScope yet
QC batch expectations exist as dataset assertions; production-grade batch QC evaluation is not implemented
COA, EDD, narrative, custody appendix, and attachment-manifest artifacts are not yet generated from migrated data
subcontract-blocking, microbiology holding-time, and release-precondition controls are expressed as checks but are not enforced by migration/report code

senaite_mapping.Client: Client organization/account plus billing/report recipient boundary; current import covers name/email only.
senaite_mapping.Contact: Client contact/report-recipient role; currently represented as expected migration gap.
senaite_mapping.Sample: SENAITE sample/AnalysisRequest specimen metadata including matrix, client sample id, received condition, and containers.
senaite_mapping.AnalysisRequest: Requested analysis set expanded from profile or ad hoc services per sample.
senaite_mapping.AnalysisService: Service/analyte with method, unit, qualifier, and reporting metadata snapshot.
senaite_mapping.Worksheet: Batch assignment and analyst work queue for wet chemistry, metals, microbiology, or narrative review.
senaite_mapping.QC: Method blank, duplicate, LCS, holding-time, subcontract, and reviewer acknowledgement controls.
senaite_mapping.Report: COA, EDD, narrative, custody appendix, and attachment manifest outputs with release provenance.
senaite_mapping.ChainOfCustody: Received/split/transferred/reviewed/reported custody event timeline with actor and timestamp expectations.

assertions: all three synthetic clients import through ImportForScope
legacy_id values remain available in import row provenance
client reconciliation has zero missing and zero mismatched records
sample fixture rows include matrix, container, analyses, and custody expectations
migration docs mark sample/custody import as high severity gap
each workflow family has a QC batch
each workflow family has expected report outputs
dataset documents report package artifact gap
fixture contains no customer names or domains
all legacy ids use SYN- prefix
